pages.purpose: Page where the founder enters company and product context for the demo

important_elements.label: Product or staging URL input
important_elements.why: Founders provide the URL of the product or staging environment here

important_elements.label: Sandbox credentials input
important_elements.why: Founders enter sandbox credentials needed for the demo

important_elements.label: Target persona input
important_elements.why: Defines the buyer persona for the demo

important_elements.label: Demo goals input
important_elements.why: Founders specify what the demo should show

important_elements.label: Short walkthrough input
important_elements.why: Founders provide a short walkthrough of the demo

important_elements.label: Approved Q&A input
important_elements.why: Approved questions and answers for the AI demo agent

important_elements.label: Call to action input
important_elements.why: Defines the call to action shown to prospects

important_elements.label: Qualification questions input
important_elements.why: Questions to qualify leads during the demo

allowed_actions.label: Go to AI agent/content console
allowed_actions.intent: Show how AI output is generated from founder context

pages.purpose: Console where AI output is generated from founder-provided context

important_elements.label: AI output panel
important_elements.why: Displays AI-generated demo content and responses

important_elements.label: AI prompt input
important_elements.why: Founder can enter prompts to generate AI output

important_elements.label: Go to prospect demo room
important_elements.why: Navigate to the prospect-facing demo room

allowed_actions.label: Go to prospect demo room
allowed_actions.intent: Show the prospect demo room where buyers can ask questions

pages.purpose: Prospect demo room where buyers can interact with the AI demo agent

important_elements.label: Buyer question input
important_elements.why: Buyer can ask questions to the AI demo agent here

important_elements.label: Chat history panel
important_elements.why: Shows conversation between buyer and AI demo agent

important_elements.label: View CRM summary and follow-up
important_elements.why: Navigate to lead qualification and follow-up output

allowed_actions.label: Go to CRM summary and follow-up
allowed_actions.intent: Show lead qualification and follow-up output

pages.purpose: CRM summary page showing lead qualification and follow-up output

important_elements.label: Lead qualification panel
important_elements.why: Displays answers to qualification questions and lead status

important_elements.label: Follow-up output panel
important_elements.why: Shows follow-up messages and next steps

important_elements.label: Book a founder onboarding call button
important_elements.why: Call to action for founders to book onboarding

allowed_actions.label: Highlight book onboarding call button
allowed_actions.intent: Emphasize the call to action to book a founder onboarding call

flows.goal: Guide a founder through setting up and showing the AI demo

steps.say: Start by showing how the founder enters company and product context.
Then show the agent/content console where AI output can be generated from that context.
Next, show the prospect demo room where a buyer can ask questions.
Finally, show the CRM summary and follow-up output that qualifies the lead.
Emphasize that the final product sends a prospect a personalized link and the AI demo agent guides them, and highlight the call to action to book a founder onboarding call.

approved_answers.topic: What does the founder need to provide?
approved_answers.answer: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

approved_answers.topic: Should the runtime click the real app directly?
approved_answers.answer: The MVP should use an approved manifest and visual event playback. Playwright is better for setup-time extraction and validation. Live app control can come later for approved low-risk paths.
Yes. Gemini Live or a LiveKit voice agent can call the same safe demo tools used by text: show page, move cursor, highlight, propose click, answer from knowledge, and update lead memory.

notes: All navigation actions use only routes and selectors explicitly provided or implied by founder context.
No destructive or unsafe actions are included.
Clicks are only included for clearly safe controls like navigation buttons.
Highlight actions are used to emphasize important CTAs without triggering side effects.

decisions.question: What does the founder need to provide?
decisions.reply: A founder needs to provide a product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, call to action, and qualification questions.

events.say: A founder needs to provide a product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, call to action, and qualification questions.

decisions.reason: This answer is directly from the approved Q&A in the founder input manifest.

decisions.question: Show me the agent/content console.
decisions.reply: Here is the agent/content console where AI output is generated from the founder-provided context.

events.say: Showing the agent/content console where AI output is generated from founder context.

decisions.reason: The agent console page is approved and safe to show to demonstrate AI output generation.

decisions.question: Show the prospect demo room.
decisions.reply: Now showing the prospect demo room where buyers can interact with the AI demo agent by asking questions.

events.say: Showing the prospect demo room where buyers can ask questions to the AI demo agent.

decisions.reason: The demo room page is approved and safe to show for buyer interaction demonstration.

decisions.question: How do we qualify the lead?
decisions.reply: Lead qualification is done in the CRM summary page, which displays answers to qualification questions and follow-up output. Let me show you that page.

events.say: Showing the CRM summary page with lead qualification and follow-up output.

decisions.reason: The CRM summary page is the designated place for lead qualification and follow-up, safe to show for this purpose.

success.success_rule: MVP-usable means at least 3/4 expected app areas discovered, at least 6 safe/reviewable actions, no invented manifest pages/action refs, and at least 3/4 prospect questions planned to the right page/action.
